issue_title: Remove checkpoint at epoch 0
issue_body:
If `epoch = 0` the `if` of following code is successful for any reasonable `checkpoint_step`
```python
        # Checkpoint the model
        if (epoch % checkpoint_step == 0) or (epoch == training_iters - 1):
            checkpoint_path = os.path.join(checkpoint_dir, 'model.ckpt')
            print "Checkpointing in directory", "%s" % checkpoint_dir
            saver.save(session, checkpoint_path, global_step=epoch)
            print
```
Thus the model checkpoints at `epoch = 0`.

However, a checkpoint at `epoch = 0` is basically of no use, as the model has only seen one epoch. Thus no checkpoint should occur at `epoch = 0`.

issue_title: Auth backend does not load Django's (model based) permissions
issue_body:
`OIDCAuthenticationBackend` does not subclass neither implements it's own authorization methods. As a result django (model based) permissions get completely ignored.

I suggest that `OIDCAuthenticationBackend` subclasses `django.contrib.auth.backends.ModelBackend` can get Django's default authorization methods.

While this is not technically a bug, is un-expected behavior thus I suggest that we change it.

Note that [according to the docs](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.11/topics/auth/customizing/#handling-authorization-in-custom-backends) Django will fetch permissions for user from all listed `AUTHENTICATION_BACKENDS`. So if `ModelBackend` is included in the list, the user will have model based permissions. Nevertheless I still believe that OIDC backend should provide that functionality itself so we don't have to have ModelBackend enabled as well (which may result in a security risk).

issue_title: Proposal for re-evaluating completion of ExternalTasks
issue_body:
Currently because ExternalTasks don't implement `run()`, they are marked as not runnable when the task is added. That means that they aren't assigned to a worker. As a result, the scheduler will never return a PENDING ExternalTask to a worker in `get_work`. So if an ExternalTask completes while luigi is running, it will never be detected and so dependent Tasks will not run. I want to change that.

It looks like tasks are distributed to workers before any tasks are run. So that means that multiple long running tasks could be queued to the same worker, with multiple short running tasks assigned to another worker. Therefore, we could end up with one worker doing all the long running tasks, and other workers remaining idle. This isn't ideal, so I don't want to hack the logic that queues up tasks to workers.

I suggest implementing logic to retry ExternalTasks as follows:

1. Returning the highest priority `PENDING` task from the scheduler if there is no `best_task` (subject to retry logic in 3. below).
2. Make workers call `task.complete()` if `run()` is not implemented
3. Add backoff logic with a new state (BACKED_OFF) & task attribute indicating when it entered that state. The scheduler will look at these in 1. above. There's no point hammering away at missing external tasks when there are others that could be evaluated.

If `task.complete()` returns `False`, workers will remove themselves from the task.workers set so that the task can be assigned to a different worker later. 

What are your thoughts? I don't want to do this work if it won't be merged in since we don't want to maintain our own fork.

issue_body: * Use rootUri in favour of rootPath

issue_title: Allow log level configuration with -v -vv -vvv etc
issue_body: This is much easier than falling back to a config file as we need to do now

issue_body: The tests/ directory is not distributed on https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/source/c/cloudflare/. Could you please distribute it?

- Running risk the following error is raised:

```python
[2017-03-30 18:43:06,097 #2 CRITICAL] Traceback (most recent call last):

issue_title: Trap SIGHUP signal and mark computation failed
issue_body: We should trap the `SIGHUP` signal, revoke the tasks and mark the computation failed (like a `SIGTERM`). When a calculation is launched via CLI without using `byoby`/`screen`/`tmux` or `nohup` it depends on the availability of the controlling terminal. This can disappear when it's allocated via ssh and the connection is then closed.

issue_title: unable to import billard as package when building a new amazon-ami
issue_body:
i am building a new amazon instance with python 2.7.10 as default.
after i have ran my machine provisioning scripts and the moment of truth arrives, celery gave me an import so i debugged the problem to billard.

the package appears to be in the correct path i.e.

```bash
sudo find -name "billiard"
./srv/ia-live/lib64/python2.7/dist-packages/billiard
```

where ia-live is the path of my virtualenv. checking the path in via python virtualenv execuatable

```python
import sys
sys.path

['',

i don't understand why there could be problem

issue_title: Armorable.from_blob fails if the blob is a bytes object
issue_body:
because it assumes it is a str, and this may not always be the case.

Probably the easiest fix would be to change:

```python
def from_blob(cls, blob):
    obj = cls()
    po = obj.parse(bytearray(blob, 'latin-1'))
```

to

```python
def from_blob(cls, blob):
    obj = cls()
    if isinstance(blob, six.binary_type):
        po = obj.parse(bytearray(blob))
    else:
        po = obj.parse(bytearray(blob, 'latin-1'))    
```

issue_title: Using boto3 classes in type annotations
issue_body:
Say I have a function that accepts an EC2 instance as input, and I want to represent this in my type signature. How would I do that?

Here is my first try:

```python
# Silly example
def get_ip(instance: boto3.resource('ec2').Instance) -> str:
    return instance.public_ip_address
```

This will fail the type checker since this `Instance` is a method and not a class.

```python
>>> boto3.resource('ec2').Instance
<bound method ResourceFactory._create_class_partial.<locals>.create_resource of ec2.ServiceResource()>
```

Even without the type checker -- say if I was just using this for documentation purposes -- this would fail on your build server since we didn't provide a region name for the `resource()` call.

boto3 clearly does have an `Instance` class defined somewhere inside, but it's not clear how one is supposed to access the class directly:

```python
>>> type(boto3.resource('ec2').Instance('i-someid00'))
<class 'boto3.resources.factory.ec2.Instance'>
>>> boto3.resources.factory.ec2.Instance
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
AttributeError: module 'boto3.resources.factory' has no attribute 'ec2'
```

So how does one go about using boto3 classes in their type annotations?

issue_title: Revisiting #169 - Need way to get pre-constructed, non-signed S3 signature from Resource
issue_body:
The solution in #169 is 

> You should be able to do this now by using a client configured with the signature version botocore.UNSIGNED, does that work for you?

So if I do

```
config.signature_version = botocore.UNSIGNED
boto3.client('s3', config=config).generate_presigned_url('get_object', ExpiresIn=0, Params={'Bucket': bucket, 'Key': key})
```

Everything is nice and dandy.

Now, if I use Resource instead of Client, it no longer works.

```
config.signature_version = botocore.UNSIGNED
boto3.resource('s3',config=config).meta.client.generate_presigned_url('get_object', ExpiresIn=0, Params={'Bucket': bucket, 'Key': key})
```

`generate_presigned_url` raises AttributeError `object has no attribute endswith` because it's expecting a string.

issue_title: Mismatch between map keys, documentation, and enum values
issue_body:
This issue arises in a number of service definitions, I'll use SQS as the motivating example.

In the documentation for the [Message](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/APIReference/API_Message.html) data type, the valid keys in an [`AttributeMap`](https://github.com/boto/botocore/blob/4fb1c05892234de2d6e2bac500e93b2d00df14d9/botocore/data/sqs/2012-11-05/service-2.json#L562) shape form the following set:

* `Policy`
* `VisibilityTimeout`
* `MaximumMessageSize`
* `MessageRetentionPeriod`
* `ApproximateNumberOfMessages`
* `ApproximateNumberOfMessagesNotVisible`
* `CreatedTimestamp`
* `LastModifiedTimestamp`
* `QueueArn`
* `ApproximateNumberOfMessagesDelayed`
* `DelaySeconds`
* `ReceiveMessageWaitTimeSeconds`
* `RedrivePolicy`

These keys are reflected in the SQS [service definition](https://github.com/boto/botocore/blob/4fb1c05892234de2d6e2bac500e93b2d00df14d9/botocore/data/sqs/2012-11-05/service-2.json#L1167-L1180) for the `QueueAttributeName` enumeration.

If you look at the sample response for the [ReceiveMessage](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/APIReference/API_ReceiveMessage.html) operation, it utilises keys that are not in the above set.

Question:

* Is the `ReceiveMessage` operation documentation wrong in the sample response
* Is the `Message` type documentation and service definition wrong WRT the use of `QueueAttributeName` for attribute keys?

issue_title: Is profile prefix deprecated or required?
issue_body:
You folks say that the profile prefix is required:
https://github.com/boto/botocore/blob/d00ee27528efad227ad46d3db9fd2f80f802803f/botocore/configloader.py#L208-L222

AWS Java SDK says that profile prefix is deprecated:
https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-java/blame/46c00efdd9240d2d20d734f806dcc71506af6e0f/aws-java-sdk-core/src/main/java/com/amazonaws/auth/profile/internal/BasicProfileConfigLoader.java#L96-L99

Between you, it is seemingly impossible to write ~/.aws/config in a compliant way.

issue_title: date queries (last month, this month) should respect the current day
issue_body:
Currently, we support queries like:

- date today
- date yesterday
- date last month
- date this month

with variations of (-1, -2, ...).

Last month and this month queries should respect the current day. Currently they "reset" to the first day of the month.

issue_title: Add support for authenticated calls to the Spotify Web API
issue_body:
Spotify [recently announced](https://developer.spotify.com/news-stories/2017/01/27/removing-unauthenticated-calls-to-the-web-api/) the following:
> On 29th May 2017 the /search /tracks /albums /artists and /users (and related) endpoints will start requiring an access token, bringing them in-line with all other Web API endpoints

We currently access the `/search` and `/tracks` endpoints so we'll soon need to use Spotify's [Authorization Code Flow](https://developer.spotify.com/web-api/authorization-guide/#authorization_code_flow) to continue doing so. The good news is that the infrastructure for doing this has already been setup and is used by [mopidy-spotify-web](https://github.com/lfcabend/mopidy-spotify-web). Merging mopidy-spotify-web and mopidy-spotify together (https://github.com/mopidy/mopidy-spotify/issues/114) makes even more sense and might be the first place to start.

We would then be able to start using the Web API for more of the features currently provided by libspotify and reduce our dependency on that deprecated library (https://github.com/mopidy/mopidy-spotify/issues/110). Except, of course, for streaming which is unfortunately still only possible through libspotify. 

The Web API also give us access to features/improvements that are not available in libspotify, such as:
* 'Your Music' (https://github.com/mopidy/mopidy-spotify/issues/16).
* 'Top Lists' / Discover Weekly / Release Radar (https://github.com/mopidy/mopidy-spotify/issues/122)
* Playlist images (https://github.com/mopidy/mopidy-spotify/issues/100)
* Better searching (https://github.com/mopidy/mopidy-spotify/issues/11)

issue_title: stop using multiprocessing
issue_body:
Several interesting issues have arisen in my many hours fighting multiprocessing. The most obvious is the issue with forwarding stdin to the subprocess which requires terrible hacks, especially on windows.

However another much more subtle issue is this... on unix multiprocessing by default does not fork+exec. It only forks. This is interesting because it uses the original python interpreter. The issue is that any libraries that were already imported are not re-imported into the child process because they are already found in memory. Instead we would prefer to fork+exec a new subprocess which uses its own interpreter to get a fresh system. To be more clear, if you import a library prior to `hupper.start_reloader` and then you try to develop on that library, it will not be reloaded even though hupper says it restarted.

issue_title: tchannel-python assumes any incoming connections with host:port in init headers can be used to send RPCs
issue_body:
When TChannel receives an incoming connection, it receives init headers which include a host:port that represents whether the other side is listening.

tchannel-python currently assumes that any connection which has a host:port is OK to send RPCs back on. It previously also sent RPCs to connections that were marked ephemeral, but [that was fixed](https://github.com/uber/tchannel-python/pull/449).

If any service connects to a tchannel-python service, RPCs may end up going back over that incoming connection, and typically the client receives a `BadRequest` error (because the remote host:port was not expecting those RPCs). These requests are not retried, and cause an immediate failure.

issue_title: Undelete does nothing when using DELETED_INVISIBLE
issue_body:
This is actually a pre-existing one I've been running into on Django 1.7/1.8.

When attempting to undelete (save) a deleted model, the `_base_manager` is used by Django to create the "base_qs" to perform the update from:

```python
base_qs = cls._base_manager.using(using)
...
updated = self._do_update(base_qs, using, pk_val, values, update_fields, forced_update)
```

Because the _base_manager is the SafeDeleteManager, only model instances where deleted__isnull=True are considered. This returns and empty set of course because we're attempting to save an instance where deleted is not null.

As a result, `_do_update` returns False, since no records were updated, and django then attempts to insert a new record, which defeats the purpose of undeleting.

So far, the only workaround I've found is to set `_base_manager = models.Manager()`, but this has some other negative side-effects, such as causing reverse managers from instances with ForeignKeys to the SafeDelete model to not filter out deleted models.

The first migration create the field unique=True and every next migration is trying to set unique to False. And over and over.
Here is patch:

```
diff --git a/audit_log/models/managers.py b/audit_log/models/managers.py
index ee3defd..1c04825 100644
--- a/audit_log/models/managers.py
+++ b/audit_log/models/managers.py
@@ -101,6 +101,9 @@ class AuditLog(object):
 
                     field.__class__ = models.IntegerField
 
+                if isinstance(field, models.OneToOneField):
+                    field.__class__ = models.ForeignKey
+
                 if field.primary_key:
                     field.serialize = True
 
-- 
1.7.5.4
 ```

issue_title: cscore based CameraServer
issue_body:
Here's how I think this will probably work:

* The CameraServer class will live in a separate pip package called robotpy-cameraserver 
* It'll wrap the real cscore with cython, and provide a mechanism to carry the opencv images in any callbacks over to python land, the same way that mjpg-streamer does it
* Because this is python, I think we should launch the camera stuff via multiprocessing -- this will allow the user to upload their camera code via pyfrc's installer, and avoid the GIL contention if we just used threads instead
* A requirement that falls out of this is that the cameraserver needs to detect if HAL or WPILib is imported, and if it is to immediately fail (not sure how existing imports work with multiprocessing, this may be tricky to actually do).
* Any image processing callback code will be required to be in its own module, because of the wpilib/hal import restriction

Some unknowns that will need to be addressed (which will need to be addressed for robotpy-ctre as well):

* I only want compilation/cython to happen on the roborio -- though really, I'd rather distribute that as a precompiled wheel package, then when you use pip on a normal system it doesn't do the compilation and simulates it instead
  * An alternative solution might have to be that the wrapper gets built separately, and is included the same way that HAL is included. It would be nice if it were compiled to a real python .so though, instead of needing ctypes... I suspect it would be difficult to share the OpenCV images via ctypes, as the Mat -> ndarray code is [~300 lines of c++](https://github.com/robotpy/mjpg-streamer/blob/master/mjpg-streamer-experimental/plugins/input_opencv/filters/cvfilter_py/conversion.cpp)

Other things I haven't thought of yet:

* At some point, cscore will work on Windows/OSX too. How do we allow people to easily test their cscore-based code on those platforms? The easy answer might be to just write a stub function that they can call in an `if __name__ == '__main__'` block, ala `wpilib.run`.

issue_title: pipenv client can't report some errors when importing requirements
issue_body:
`pipenv install` suffers an exception when trying to report on failures to import a requirements.txt.

This is under python3.

For example, to reproduce, under python3.6:

```
$ cat req.txt
-e thing

$ pipenv install -r req.txt
Requirements file provided! Importing into Pipfile…
Traceback (most recent call last):
  ...<snip>...
  File "/Users/dsantibanez/Library/Python/3.6/lib/python/site-packages/pipenv/cli.py", line 1813, in install
    if error and e:
UnboundLocalError: local variable 'e' referenced before assignment
```

This is because some pipenv cli `except` clauses are shadowing a variable, and their `finally` is expecting to be able to use that variable.

https://github.com/pypa/pipenv/blob/50ad7746eebb397e97d9403456921743d2ffb3bb/pipenv/cli.py#L1795
https://github.com/pypa/pipenv/blob/50ad7746eebb397e97d9403456921743d2ffb3bb/pipenv/cli.py#L1799
https://github.com/pypa/pipenv/blob/50ad7746eebb397e97d9403456921743d2ffb3bb/pipenv/cli.py#L1804
https://github.com/pypa/pipenv/blob/50ad7746eebb397e97d9403456921743d2ffb3bb/pipenv/cli.py#L1813

By line 1813, `e` is out of scope.

issue_title: prepare_pip_source_args doesn't properly handle URLs w/ user & password
issue_body:
When the I use a custom source in the Pipfile that is protected by a user & password. 

The [logic in prepare_pip_source_args](https://github.com/pypa/pipenv/blob/2282e7045f08c0794973d296b11ea174e7fda48a/pipenv/utils.py#L268) tries to manually parse ParseResult.netloc (returned from urlparse) this results in the username being passed as the `--trusted-host` when `verify_ssl` is set to false in the Pipfile.

For example, if one of the sources in the Pipfile is `https://user:password@custom.example.com/simple`  
it will result calling `resolver.py` with the wrong params (passed via the PIPENV_PACKAGES environment variable) and called from venv_resolve_deps
the value of the ` --trusted-host` will be the username instead of the host name.

I am adding a [PR](https://github.com/pypa/pipenv/pull/1774) with tests to demonstrate & fix the issue.

issue_title: `pipenv update --dev <specific_package>` doesn't forward '--dev' to install
issue_body:
Updating a dev package leads to it being added to the non-dev section. This is because `ctx.invoke(install, ...)` doesn't automatically forward the `dev=True` flag.

For instance, running `pipenv update --dev mock` in the `pipenv` repo causes `mock` to be moved from `dev-packages` to `packages`.

Best of all would be if the `--dev` flag wasn't necessary for update, it instead keeping packages in place.

```
diff --git a/pipenv/cli.py b/pipenv/cli.py
index 81bf29e..8ad1901 100644
--- a/pipenv/cli.py
+++ b/pipenv/cli.py
@@ -2617,7 +2617,7 @@ def update(ctx, dev=False, three=None, python=None, dry_run=False, bare=False, d
                 # sys.exit(1)

             p_name = convert_deps_to_pip({package_name: project.all_packages[package_name]}, r=False)
-            ctx.invoke(install, package_name=p_name[0])
+            ctx.invoke(install, package_name=p_name[0], dev=dev, verbose=verbose)

         else:
             click.echo(
```

##### Describe your environment

1. OS Type: macOS 10.13
1. Python version: 3.6.4
1. Pipenv version: 9.0.1 (and d8dc25b)

issue_title: package install ends with temp files not deleted
issue_body:
When `pipenv install` finishes installing packages there are leftovers in `/tmp/pipenv-xxxxx-requirement.txt` (one per package installed).

##### Describe your environment

1. `Linux 4.13.0-36-generic #40-Ubuntu SMP x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux`
2. Python version: tested with 2.7.14 and 3.6.4
3. Pipenv version: 10.1.2

##### Expected result

no tmp files left

##### Actual result

many tmp files in /tmp 

##### Steps to replicate

just install any package

The issue is caused IMO by running pipenv concurrently, the tmp file cannot be immediately deleted [at this point](https://github.com/pypa/pipenv/blob/5ba8c9cf2484d257c21ded9eeca7f981bc911d0a/pipenv/core.py#L1465). 

I think we can add a small cleanup task similar to `pipenv.utils.rmtree()` at `pipenv/core.py +890` when we're sure that the job is finished.
A couple of options for this:

1) `pipenv.core.pip_install()` returns also a list of files that has used but not deleted. Our cleanup task takes it as input and does the cleanup at the very end of `pipenv.core.do_install_dependencies()` before returning.

2) Or  one can amend the `project.Project` adding the temp dir where the requirement files are created so the cleanup task can be executed with something like `rmtree(project.tmp_requirements_location)`.

Any thoughts on this issue?

If you agree with the proposed solution, I can submit a patch for evaluation. 

Thanks!

issue_title: find_packages() not compatible with PEP420 namespaces
issue_body:
Originally reported by: **wichert (Bitbucket: [wichert](http://bitbucket.org/wichert), GitHub: [wichert](http://github.com/wichert))**

----------------------------------------

I ran into a problem with a package always generating empty wheels. This turned out to be caused by setuptools.find_packages not finding any packages when using PEP 420 namespaces. This is easy to test:

```
$ mkdir -p src/foo/bar
$ : > src/foo/bar/__init__.py
$ python  -c 'from setuptools import find_packages ; print(find_packages("src"))'
[]
```

Adding an empty __init__.py to quickly simulate old-style namespace packages makes find_packages do the right thing:

```
$ : > src/foo/__init__.py
$ ../bin/python  -c 'from setuptools import find_packages ; print(find_packages("src"))'
['foo', 'foo.bar']
```

----------------------------------------
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/pypa/setuptools/issue/431

issue_title: please implement PEP 503
issue_body:
as specified in [python.org/dev/peps](https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0503/#normalized-names).
We are using nexus to deploy our inhouse packages but they can't be found because nexus already implemented PEP 503.
So when e.g. the python pakage name is `my.pkg` after twine (or setup.py sdist) upload it appears as `my-pkg` in the nexus index and buildout fails (`Error: Couldn't find a distribution for 'my.pkg==1.0a1'`.)
The Error occurs in 

> def find_packages(self, requirement):
>    ...
>    for url in list(self.package_pages.get(requirement.key, ())):
>             # scan each page that might be related to the desired package
>             self.scan_url(url)

and currently we have to patch around with `self.scan_url(url.replace('my.', 'my-'))`

Please see related topics at [https://github.com/pypa/pip/issues/3666](https://github.com/pypa/pip/issues/3666) and [https://issues.sonatype.org/browse/NEXUS-12501](https://issues.sonatype.org/browse/NEXUS-12501)

issue_title: Spurious "Provides-Extra:" generated in metadata
issue_body:
In the recently released version of setuptools, when installing my distribution [copyingmock](https://pypi.python.org/packages/e7/4b/857c745721ed3d5dc33c426d6e31aaff13a5cd5caaf444117139b2d9936f/copyingmock-0.2.tar.gz#md5=9aa6ba13542d25e527fe358d53cdaf3b), it claims that it provides the `extras=['']`.  Of course, I don't provide the empty-string extra, and the correct metadata should be `extras=[]`.  This seems to be related to [using the conditional deps feature](https://github.com/wimglenn/copyingmock/blob/c80ec21e80a733138b99eed6020563ceae8c20ef/setup.py#L26), somehow:

    install_requires=["mock ; python_version<'3.3'"],

To reproduce the issue, you can get the sdist:

    $ pip download copyingmock==0.2 --no-binary copyingmock

Then use `git bisect` with this script:

```
#!/usr/bin/env python
# check.py
import json
import subprocess
import sys
from zipfile import ZipFile

if __name__ == '__main__':
    subprocess.check_call(['pip', 'wheel', '--no-deps', '--no-index', './copyingmock-0.2.tar.gz'])
    whl = ZipFile('copyingmock-0.2-py2.py3-none-any.whl')
    metadata = json.loads(whl.read('copyingmock-0.2.dist-info/metadata.json'))
    print(metadata['extras'])

    if metadata['extras']:
        sys.exit(1)
    else:
        sys.exit(0)
```

I did bisect and found 

    $ git bisect start
    $ git bisect bad tags/v38.6.0
    $ git bisect good tags/v28.8.0
    Bisecting: 375 revisions left to test after this (roughly 9 steps)
    [2cd7f22eb62b413d65fe4208d66f7e2738f71a56] Correctly honor exist_ok. Ref #1083.
    $ git bisect run ./check.py
    ...

https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/commit/b2ea3c4a20d008622caec445f5b6916ddd420d16 is the first bad commit.

issue_title: Handle comments inside script tags
issue_body:
Currently, the parser ignores HTML comments inside script tags, treating them as part of the text element:

```
>>> import parsel
>>> parsel.__version__
'1.0.2'
>>>
>>> # comments inside random nodes...
>>> node_with_comments = parsel.Selector(u'<node><!-- comment -->text here</node>')
>>> node_with_comments.xpath('//comment()')
[<Selector xpath='//comment()' data=u'<!-- comment -->'>]
>>> node_with_comments.xpath('//text()')
[<Selector xpath='//text()' data=u'text here'>]
>>> # okay, looks good
>>>
>>> # now, with comments inside a script tag:
>>> script_with_comments = parsel.Selector(u'<script><!-- comment -->alert("hello")</script>')
>>> script_with_comments.xpath('//comment()')
[]
>>> # oops, can't find the comments!
>>> script_with_comments.xpath('//text()')
[<Selector xpath='//text()' data=u'<!-- comment -->alert("hello")'>]
```
Looks like it ignore comments inside the `<script>` tag, considering it all part of the text.

This is a problem because people are unable to easily strip HTML comments from the JavaScript code (which is often fed to a JS parser, like js2xml).

Changing this would break backwards compatibility, but this looks like a bug to me.

Thoughts? Concerns?

issue_title: message.value is None, stability and other possible problems
issue_body:
Hi, 

These are some problems that we meet using pykafka consumer. Even though, there are some other tools in the chain, I have a feeling that the reason is in pykafka (using the 2.1.2 release). No proofs, unfortunately, this is just for the record. 

### m.value is None
Sometimes, when I do  m= message.cosume(), the m.value is None, while None is never posted to kafka. 
Related thing: we have several consumers (with different consumer group) in parallel on the topic. One started to get None values (because it tried to parse the m.value), the other forwarded the messages (no matter its content) via bruce to another kafka, and the (pykafka) consumer there did not say anything about None messages (at least, it seems so). 

### Failed to acquire partition
We have two consumers running from the one consumer group, treating each two partitions 

One of them got  stuck for some reasons, and the other could not acquire partitions for ~12 hours, 
the code was trying to recreate consumer with 1 seconds delay, but every time got this error:
```
16-01-17 02:45:20,633 - pykafka.balancedconsumer - WARNING - Failed to acquire partition <pykafka.partition.Partition at 0x7f1dd195abd0 (id=2)> after 4 retries.
2016-01-17 02:45:20,634 - pykafka.balancedconsumer - ERROR - Stopping consumer in response to error
2016-01-17 02:45:20,642 - kafkarecon.kafkarecon - ERROR - Got an exception! ConsumerStoppedException()
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/kafka2aero/kafkarecon/kafkarecon.py", line 98, in start
    m = self.consumer.consume()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pykafka/balancedconsumer.py", line 694, in consume
    raise ConsumerStoppedException
ConsumerStoppedException
```
thus, downtime for 12h.

### repeated message reception

We have such a dataflow   php -> bruce (kafka producer daemon) -> kafka -> pykafka balacned consumer -> bruce -> kafka (another one) -> pykafka balanced consumer -> mysql. 

php puts json messages with unique values in field id.  Never None (see the first issue). So, at the end, last pykafka consumer receives (rarely, but it does) repeated messages, thus, getting duplicate on primary key exception with mysql.  I logged the values, so it's really repeated message   kafka->mysql script tries to put in mysql. Here you could blame any of the tools (and arrows) in the chain, but I think it can be related to pykafka.


Any enlightenments or guesses are welcome

Thanks

issue_title: Add ability to swallow I/O errors
issue_body:
When monitoring streamparse topologies with tools like Sentry, receiving tons of errors like:

```
Received EOF while trying to read stdin from Storm, pipe appears to be broken, exiting.
Error streamparse.storm.component in read_messagestreamparse.ipc
```

Lead to alerting fatigue (we've had 5.9K of these in just one of our production topologies, as @dan-blanchard knows :)). The IPC layer shutting down like this, is usually because of another issue like some other component dying and Storm killing components or rebalancing a topology. In the former case, the actual exception is captured elsewhere already, in the latter, it's a non-error. Either way, the utility of the IPC exception is questionable.

My gut instinct is just swallow these exceptions full stop, but maybe others have use for them. Ideas on how to handle the ability to stop these from being raised?

issue_title: How to cutout mask from an image
issue_body:
Consider the following example

```
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
from regions import CirclePixelRegion, PixCoord
center=PixCoord(10,10)
circle=CirclePixelRegion(center, 5)
fig = plt.figure()
ax = fig.add_subplot(1, 1, 1)
np.random.seed(12345)
data=np.random.random((20,20))
mask=circle.to_mask()
data[mask.bbox.slices] *= 1 - mask.data
ax.imshow(data, interpolation='nearest', cmap='viridis')
ax.add_patch(circle.as_patch(facecolor='none', edgecolor='red'))
```
![figure_1](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/11678435/26497648/7ddbe456-422c-11e7-9b17-2edbf32e250d.png)

This is a common use case I have, and also what I would have expected that [Mask.cutout(image)](http://astropy-regions.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api/regions.Mask.html#regions.Mask.cutout) would do. It doesn't, which I guess is fine since many/most other people will need the functionality that is currently implemented.

Do agree that my use case also be added to the API somehow? I suggest

```
mask.cutout_from(image)
```
Or is this too confusing?

issue_title: docker: support for boot2docker
issue_body:
In general the new docker+docker compose works very nicely. I've tested it on OS X with boot2docker and found some few issues.

**Problem 1** 
Boot2docker creates a minimal VirtualBox VM with docker installed on it and pipes all docker commands to that VM. 
Docker-compose bridges the containers ports to the localhost network interface on the docker host, which in case of boot2docker is localhost on the VM. This means that none of the ports are open to the VM host system (OS X in this case). 

The fix is quite easy as provided by @crepererum:

```
ports:
    - "127.0.0.1:5000:5000"
```
->
```
ports:
    - "5000:5000"
```

It makes sense when the docker host is also the developer machine that the docker containers ports are not opened to your entire LAN. So not sure what the best fix is here. Perhaps just add documentation for boot2docker or provide two different docker-compose files. 

**Problem 2**
Given that problem 1 is fixed, then there's still a problem that ``CFG_SITE_URL`` is set to ``http://localhost:5000`` in ``docker_boot.sh``. Perhaps the fix here is just to ingest ``CFG_SITE_URL`` 
via env variables similar to e.g. ``CFG_DATABASE_HOST``.

issue_title: config: default ratelimits warning
issue_body:
Current configuration for `Flask-Limiter`, is basically the in-memory storage opiton, which applies on a per-process level, effectively allowing a much greater limit compared to what is configured. 

The default of `RATELIMIT_DEFAULT = '5000/hour'`, in a production Invenio deployment running a 4-process uWSGI config across 2 web nodes would result to a total of `4 * 4 * 5,000 = 80,000 reqs/hour` distributed randomly across the 16 processes.

Possible solutions:

1. Warning when the in-memory storage option is being used
2. Lower default ratelimit (thought that wouldn't help mitigate a 
3. `setup.py` with `extra_require['redis']` and "feature-detection" in `init_app`

issue_title: global: celery v4.2.0 breaks task discovery
issue_body:
Because the extension is calling `celery.autodiscover_tasks` with an empty string (`''`) as the `related_name` argument, this [line eventually fails in celery](https://github.com/celery/celery/blob/9778f2bfe0bf0c4f3d554bdcb479ad769a349fc2/celery/loaders/base.py#L263) (responsible commit: https://github.com/WoaDmulL/celery/commit/c089f86e338c852043bc2ee25be2b82fd53a45a6).

Since it's not very convenient to change all of the invenio package entrypoints to support a `invenio_package` + `tasks` naming convention, there's two solutions:

1. Pin `celery` to `<4.2`
2. Wait until the issue is resolved upstream

issue_title: Request is not logged to sentry using the default django middleware
issue_body:
It looks like there's code to automatically track requests and send them to sentry even when you don't pass `extra={'request': request}` to the logging call. However, it's not working for us because of the following:

The request gets set in local thread storage [here](https://github.com/getsentry/raven-python/blob/6ead8beb09d6d40b91be213580dd4d9d5bd72f37/raven/contrib/django/middleware/__init__.py#L105), where `self.thread` is an instance of `raven.contrib.django.middleware.SentryMiddleware`, and then read [here](https://github.com/getsentry/raven-python/blob/6ead8beb09d6d40b91be213580dd4d9d5bd72f37/raven/contrib/django/client.py#L258), where `SentryLogMiddleware.thread` is a `property` object, which is not what it was set on, and thus leads to `request` being `None`.

Doing some git blaming, I think this broke in https://github.com/getsentry/raven-python/commit/c3e504a0bf77deb9a79bd841e9cacc62c8127901, where `SentryMiddleware.thread` was changed from a thread-local class variable to a property

This is with raven-python 5.32.0

issue_title: What is this comment in the code talking about?
issue_body:
[This passage in the code](https://github.com/getsentry/raven-python/blob/0c417999944646c0d8d27856f2440ecb6b1c336a/raven/breadcrumbs.py#L145-L155) reads as follows:

```python
        # We do not want to include exc_info as argument because it often
        # lies (set to a constant value like 1 or True) or even if it's a
        # tuple it will not be particularly useful for us as we cannot
        # process it anyways.
        kwargs.pop('exc_info', None)
        data.update({
            'message': formatted_msg,
            'category': logger.name,
            'level': logging.getLevelName(level).lower(),
            'data': kwargs,
        })
```

First,

> We do not want to include `exc_info` as argument because it often lies (set to a constant value like 1 or True)

`exc_info` is not "lying"; 1/`True` is a documented, valid value: it means to retrieve the exception information from `sys.exc_info`. See [`logging.debug`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/logging.html#logging.Logger.debug):

> If exc\_info does not evaluate as false, it causes exception information to be added to the logging message. If an exception tuple (in the format returned by `sys.exc_info()`) or an exception instance is provided, it is used; otherwise, `sys.exc_info()` is called to get the exception information.

The passage continues,

> or even if it's a tuple it will not be particularly useful for us as we cannot process it anyways

Why not? How does `sentry_client.captureException()` manage to capture the exception information, and why can't this code path use similar means?

We see a number of Sentry calls that end up originating from this area; they come from an incredibly generic area of our code unfortunately, so the exception information would be extremely useful in determining _what_ went wrong.

issue_title: AWS Lambda uses ThreadedHTTPTransport instead of HTTPTransport
issue_body:
In AWS Lambda environment, `HTTPTransport` should be used instead of the default `ThreadedHTTPTransport` (this was added in #828). Which HTTP transport to use is [determined](https://github.com/getsentry/raven-python/blob/master/raven/conf/remote.py#L27) based on the presence of the `LAMBDA_TASK_ROOT` environment variable. But in my case `ThreadedHTTPTransport` is used, even though `LAMBDA_TASK_ROOT` is there.

I discovered this when noticing that some of the errors were not being reported to Sentry.

**Why this happens?**

It seems that when instantiating the `Client` from DSN, the default transport [is used](https://github.com/getsentry/raven-python/blob/master/raven/conf/remote.py#L98), and the one determined by [discover_default_transport](https://github.com/getsentry/raven-python/blob/master/raven/conf/remote.py#L17) is ignored.

**How to fix this?**

The `discover_default_transport` should be taken into account when using [RemoteConfig.from_string](https://github.com/getsentry/raven-python/blob/master/raven/conf/remote.py#L79). If this is not acceptable, the docs should mention that `transport` needs to be manually set to `raven.transport.http.HTTPTransport` when using Sentry on AWS Lambda.

issue_title: Implement Frame construction from a list of dictionaries
issue_body:
A common type of JSON serialization is to convert each row in the dataset into a dictionary, and then collect all these dictionaries into a list of rows. We should support constructing a Frame from such a source.

There are 2 modes of interest here:
* If `names` parameter is specified explicitly, then it dictates the number of columns in the Frame being constructed. Each row may have more or fewer keys than given by the `names`, which should result in extra values being dropped, and missing values replaced with NAs.
* If `names` parameter is not given, then the set of column names should be determined as a union of all keys from all rows.

The tortuosity can be computed since the features involved (section path and radial distances) already exist in NeuroM. But if and only if their values are in an one-to-one correspondence. Are they?

issue_title: Mark required fields as required in the help text
issue_body:
As an observation about usability, it's hard to use the help to build a complete command because it's tricky to know which fields are required. For instance, if you try to create a `job_template` without all the required fields:

```bash
$ tower-cli job_template create --name=hi
Error: Missing required fields: inventory, project, playbook, credential
```

Those are a lot of fields, but typing:

```
$ tower-cli job_template create --help
```

gives no indication about which fields are required.

My humble suggestion is to make a change along the lines of the following, added to `base.py`.

```python
                        # Apply the option to the method.
                        option_help = field.help
                        if field.required:
                            option_help = '[required] ' + option_help
                        click.option(
                            *args,
                            default=field.default if not ignore_defaults
                            else None,
                            help=option_help,
                            type=field.type,
                            show_default=field.show_default
                        )(new_method)
```

This will add the text "[required]" to the start of all help text messages for required fields. For example, in the case of a job template, the help text will read like the following:

    Options:
      --name TEXT                     [required] The name field.
      --description TEXT              The description field.
      --job-type [run|check]          [required] The job_type field.  [default:
                                      run]
      --inventory INVENTORY           [required] The inventory field.
      --project PROJECT               [required] The project field.
      --playbook TEXT                 [required] The playbook field.
      --machine-credential CREDENTIAL
                                      [required] The machine_credential field.
      --cloud-credential CREDENTIAL   The cloud_credential field.
      --forks INTEGER                 The forks field.
      --limit TEXT                    The limit field.

Clearly this isn't difficult to implement, but the question is whether this is a valid improvement or just more terminal clutter. We also need to write tests to for the change. I also would like to get some feedback about how to format the text. Should it be capitalized, like "[REQUIRED]"? Should it go in a different place, like at the end of the text? Maybe it would be better to just list the required fields at the very start of the help text (similar to the existing "missing required fields" error)?

issue_title: Inventory in workflow schema uses unique ID
issue_body:
The issue I'm running into is that the schema's for workflows use a unique ID for inventories (not for job_templates). This creates a problem, since the ID is unique and created when the inventory is created. So, if you intend to automatically create an inventory and then use it in a workflow, you don't know what the inventory ID is easily.

Here is an example of a schema:

- job_template: aws-infrastructure
  success_nodes:
  - inventory_source: aws (aws-inventory - 59)
    success_nodes:
    - job_template: aws-openshift-install

It would be so much easier if you could refer to an inventory by it's name and not have the " -59" in there.

Here is a bunch of background that led to me realizing the issue: https://github.com/strategicdesignteam/openshift-ansible-contrib/tree/master/reference-architecture/ansible-tower-integration

issue_title: django.conf.urls.patterns is deprecated in Django 1.10
issue_body:
I have not performed a full test yet, but following changes seems to fix a startup error when running in Django 1.10

translation_manager/admin.py (function get_urls):

1. before:

```
    def get_urls(self):
        try:
            from django.conf.urls import patterns, url
```

after:

```
    def get_urls(self):
        try:
            from django.conf.urls import url
```
2. before:

```
        urls = patterns('',
            url(r'^make/$', wrap(self.make_translations_view), name='%s_%s_make' % info),
            url(r'^compile/$', wrap(self.compile_translations_view), name='%s_%s_compile' % info),
            url(r'^load_from_po/$', wrap(self.load_from_po_view), name='%s_%s_load' % info),
        )
```

after:

```
        urls = [
            url(r'^make/$', wrap(self.make_translations_view), name='%s_%s_make' % info),
            url(r'^compile/$', wrap(self.compile_translations_view), name='%s_%s_compile' % info),
            url(r'^load_from_po/$', wrap(self.load_from_po_view), name='%s_%s_load' % info),
        ]
```

issue_title: Allow reference attributes on VALUE parameter nodes
issue_body:
The `reference` attribute allows to justify some parameter values by linking to an external reference. It is currently [allowed on `NODE`, `CODE`, `BAREME`](https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-core/blob/master/openfisca_core/legislation.xsd#L27)… but [not on `VALUE`](https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-core/blob/master/openfisca_core/legislation.xsd#L45-L48) nodes.
This prevents reusers from specifying a `reference` on a `VALUE` node (see https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/pull/789). There would be a strong value in allowing references at such a level. This would be forward-compatible with #523, that already transforms `reference` attributes to properties, and whose format explicitly defines `reference` properties on values.

It would possibly require adapting the current transformations in https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-france/pull/798, though.

issue_title: Déplacer le variables liées au logement du Ménage vers la Famille ?
issue_body:
Aujourd'hui, les variables liées au logement comme `loyer` ou `statut_occupation_logement` sont toutes déclarées pour l'entité Ménage.
Le raisonnement étant, je crois, qu'un ménage désigne des personnes qui vivent toutes sous le même toit, est donc que c'est l'entité "naturelle" pour décrire le logement.

Dans la pratique, je constate que ce choix, du moins côté prestation sociales, n'a que des inconvénients. Par exemple, parmi les formules qui utilisent ces variables (sur tout le périmètre openfisca-france), toutes **sans exception** castent leur valeur vers l'entité famille.

En pratique, cela ne facilite pas la contribution. Les entités sont un facteur de complexité pour les nouveau contributeurs. Introduire les idées de Familles et d'Individu est nécessaire pour coder les textes de lois, mais la différence entre une Famille et un Ménage est une subtilité conceptuellement non nécessaire pour la grande majorité des contributeurs, qui ont pourtant bien besoin d'accéder à des informations de bases telles que le loyer.

Je propose donc, dans la PR à venir sur le refactor de la gestion des entités, de migrer toutes les variables au logement vers l'entité Famille. Est-ce que ça vous paraît acceptable ? Est-ce qu'il me manque un élément qui pousserait vers le maintien dans l'entité Ménage ?

Merci ! 

@benjello @cbenz

issue_title: No bounds checking in pickneglogsoftmax (Python, CPU)
issue_body:
Hello,

When `pickneglogsoftmax` or `pickneglogsoftmax_batch` is called with an invalid index, rather than throwing an error, it returns a garbage value. To illustrate:

```
>>> import dynet
[dynet] random seed: 2458451077
[dynet] allocating memory: 512MB
[dynet] memory allocation done.
>>> v = dynet.inputVector([1, 2, 3])
>>> v[0].value()
1.0
>>> v[100].value()
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "_dynet.pyx", line 1666, in _dynet.Expression.__getitem__
IndexError: Index too large: 100 > 2
>>> dynet.pickneglogsoftmax(v, 0).value()
2.4076058864593506
>>> dynet.pickneglogsoftmax(v, 100).value()
3.4076058864593506
```

This can lead to serious bugs which are hard to detect. Sometimes the value returned is negative -- I only noticed my bug when my loss function was somehow dropping _below_ 0.

I'm running on CPU (I haven't tried reproducing this on GPU yet).

issue_title: Test for a low ratio of direct metabolites in the biomass reaction
issue_body:
#### Problem description

> Some biomass precursors are taken from the media and directly consumed by the biomass reaction. It might not be a problem for some ions or metabolites with known auxotrophy, but when there are too many of these metabolites, it points to artefacts of gap-filling. This mostly happens in case of automatically-generated models. Starting with a complicated automatically generated biomass formulation followed by gap-filling (which basically tries to minimise the added reactions), would lead to many biomass metabolites being "direct mets". It might be useful to report the ratio of biomass metabolites that are direct mets.
Parizad Babayi, comment on the manuscript.

issue_title: Adding batch image processing to Vision API
issue_body:
The amount of data that Vision can return can be pretty deep and processing more than one image at a time adds another level to that.

Here's my proposed approach to work with multiple images with multiple feature types.

```python
        >>> images = client.batch()
        >>> for i in range(10):
        ...     images.add_image(client.image(source_uri='gs://%s.jpg' % (i,)))
        >>> images.detect_faces()
        {'0.jpg': [], '1.jpg': [],}
        >>>
        >>> # With multiple feature types.
        >>> features = [Feature(FeatureTypes.FACE_DETECTION, 5),
        ...             Feature(FeatureTypes.LOGO_DETECTION, 3)]
        >>> for i in range(10):
        ...     image = client.image(source_uri='gs://%s.jpg' % (i,))
        ...     images.add_image(image=image, features=features)
        >>> images.detect()
```

With a context manager it could look something like this...

```python
        >>> features = [Feature(FeatureTypes.FACE_DETECTION, 5),
        ...             Feature(FeatureTypes.LOGO_DETECTION, 3)]
        >>> with client.images() as images:
        ...     for i in range(10):
        ...         image = client.image(source_uri='gs://%s.jpg' % (i,))
        ...         images.add_image(image=image, features=features)
        ...     results = images.detect()
```

issue_title: [Request] Verify User Project methods comments are not ambiguous
issue_body:
General Request:

Verify that the comments are clear for methods which accept User Project with and without Requester Pays.

Here's a distinction between Requester Pays and User Project:
User Project: Is a request metadata field that is used to assign Storage operational costs to a specified project.

Requester Pays: Is a bucket-level metadata field that is used to enforce the use of the User Project metadata field to assign operational costs when an operation is made on a Bucket and its objects.

issue_title: Datastore: unexpected keyword arg in DS client
issue_body:
Commit 9246de7a7138fabb3f25dceb50715de445684323 introduced following bug:
```
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/google/cloud/datastore/client.py", line 321, in get
    eventual=eventual)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/google/cloud/datastore/client.py", line 376, in get_multi
    transaction_id=transaction and transaction.id,
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/google/cloud/datastore/client.py", line 138, in _extended_lookup
    keys=key_pbs,
TypeError: lookup() got an unexpected keyword argument 'project_id'
```

Patch
```diff
From 7470329ecf13d9f85bfe8af71ee533856ff9ec4c Mon Sep 17 00:00:00 2001
From: mathewcohle <mathewcohle@gmail.com>
Date: Mon, 13 Nov 2017 11:41:01 +0100
Subject: [PATCH] Fix wrong keyword argument name introduced in #4348

---
 datastore/google/cloud/datastore/client.py | 2 +-
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+), 1 deletion(-)

diff --git a/datastore/google/cloud/datastore/client.py b/datastore/google/cloud/datastore/client.py
index a3d1f9d43..c42c75e32 100644
--- a/datastore/google/cloud/datastore/client.py
+++ b/datastore/google/cloud/datastore/client.py
@@ -133,7 +133,7 @@ def _extended_lookup(datastore_api, project, key_pbs,
     while loop_num < _MAX_LOOPS:  # loop against possible deferred.
         loop_num += 1
         lookup_response = datastore_api.lookup(
-            project_id=project,
+            project=project,
             read_options=read_options,
             keys=key_pbs,
         )
-- 
2.15.0
```

issue_title: Proposal for protocol enhancement
issue_body:
加入One Time Auth后的协议基本能保证CCA安全（虽然我建议直接使用一些现在被广泛采用的authenticated encryption的方法），但是仍然不能避免通过replay attack来探测的方法。举个例子，如果通过流量特征分析认为某服务器接收到的请求A与随后发出的请求B特征上有强相似，时间也重合，则可以通过重放请求A并观察服务器是否会再次发出请求B来判断A是否是对B的代理请求。
这里提出一种可能的防止replay attack以及类似的探测攻击的方法。
一般而言，防止此类攻击都是服务器会先提出一个挑战（比如签名或mac一个随机消息），客户端完成这个挑战，再进行通信。然而这在shadowsocks的设计哲学上并不适用，因为这样会导致一个麻烦的握手，协议特征也会变多。
参考[wiki](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Replay_attack)页面提出的timestamping方法，结合google的authenticater两步验证的思路，我们可以使用一种TOTP：
1. 客户端请求时取HMAC_SHA256(floor(timestamp/5000ms), somekey)的前4字节加入请求头部。
2. 服务器验证时验证最近的三个可能的MAC_SHA256(floor(timestamp/5000ms), somekey)值（这样提供一定的允许误差），如果均不符合，则拒绝请求。
这种方法要求服务端与客户端时间同步，可以通过公共的ntp服务器等等方法校准时间，手段很多就不展开了。
在时间误差内的replay attack，可以通过记录一段时间内的iv，拒绝重复的iv来防范。
这样，replay一个历史的请求就会被拒绝了。可以在日志里面警告这样的请求——这代表你的流量被他人记录了。

issue_title: Cannot add local policies to ManagedPolicyArns
Consider switching ci.sh back to pex-based execution
issue_body:
While landing #3821, the `build-support/bin/ci.sh` script was switched from (mostly) using a pants.pex to execute pants' own tests, to using the `./pants` script directly. This was primarily in order to allow the `build-support/bootstrap_native` to be invoked by `./pants`, since it propagates an anonymous/private hash version of the native binary via an environment variable.

One approach would be to re-add the ability to (optionally) run pants from a pex to the `./pants` script... this would also have the benefit of making it easier for developers to test a pants.pex locally.

issue_title: Confused weight / input with gluon.SymbolBlock visualization?
issue_body:
Hi,

I was using mxboard to visualize a symbolblock. It seems that instead of exposing inputs, the final graph exposed the weights and hid the inputs. The following codes generate the attached plots.

```python
import mxnet as mx
import mxnet.symbol as S
from mxnet import gluon
from mxboard import SummaryWriter

def _signed_embed(vocab_size, num_embed, weight_initializer=mx.init.Uniform(0.1)):
    inputs = S.var('inputs')
    label  = S.var('label')
    embed_weight = S.var('embed_weight', shape=(vocab_size, num_embed),
                           init=weight_initializer)
    embed_outputs = S.broadcast_mul(
        embed_weight.take(inputs), # batch * ... * num_embed
        label.expand_dims(-1), # batch * ... * 1
    )
    return gluon.SymbolBlock(embed_outputs, [inputs, label])
with SummaryWriter(logdir='./logs') as sw:
    sw.add_graph(_signed_embed(100,12,)(S.var('inputs'), S.var('label')))
```

The output file (ignore the binaries)

issue_title: Error while deleting backups on GCE with S3 interoperable access
issue_body:
We are using GCE in S3 interoperable access. So far all the features of WAL-E work, except delete the backups. It fails with this error. This issue was [reported before as a comment by @russss](https://github.com/wal-e/wal-e/issues/122#issuecomment-121952012) in the context of #122 , but here I want to report the specific issue with S3 protocol on GCE. ``` HINT: A better error message should be written to handle this exception. Please report this output and, if possible, the situation under which it arises. STRUCTURED: time=2015-07-30T16:26:21.219809-00 pid=21619 wal_e.retries WARNING MSG: retrying after encountering exception DETAIL: Exception information dump: Traceback (most recent call last): File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/wal_e/retries.py", line 62, in shim return f(*args, **kwargs) File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/wal_e/worker/s3/s3_deleter.py", line 27, in _delete_batch bucket.delete_keys([key.name for key in page]) File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/boto/s3/bucket.py", line 602, in delete_keys while delete_keys2(headers): File "/usr/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/boto/s3/bucket.py", line 601, in delete_keys2 body) S3ResponseError: S3ResponseError: 400 Bad Request InvalidArgumentInvalid argument.
Cannot create buckets using a POST.
 ``` We researched the issue, and we think that the root cause is that GCE does not support or implements differently [the multi-object delete api from S3](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/multiobjectdeleteapi.html). Unfortunately I was not able to find references about the support of GCE of this API. This API to delete keys in batch is used by the boto library in the function [Bucket.delete_keys()](https://github.com/boto/boto/blob/develop/boto/s3/bucket.py#L666-731) which is used by WAL-E [in the S3 Deleter()](https://github.com/wal-e/wal-e/blob/master/wal_e/worker/s3/s3_deleter.py#L27--1). This could be consider a problem in the boto library, but we can also workaround it in WAL-E by using [bucket.delete()](https://github.com/boto/boto/blob/develop/boto/s3/bucket.py#L731--1). We are going to do a corresponding PR soon.

issue_title: Unicode issues
issue_body:
In our tests, API returns encode/decode errors for the `unicode` objects when the object contains non ascii chars like 'ÖöŞşÇçİıĞğÜü', however it works fine with `unicode` objects when chars are in ascii range. 

All of the test files here on github uses typed `str` objects, so they pass. However if you make an API call using a `unicode` object, for example if you change the address as such: `u'Nidakule Göztepe, Merdivenköy Mah. Bora Sok. No:1'` it fails while building the pki string (because there are direct string casts like `str(value)`). It only fails when the object contains none ascii chars. 

We tried converting `unicode` objects to `str` before passing them to API (encoding utf-8), but we still get encode/decode errors because of the multiple casts.

In any case, nearly all of the big Python frameworks (like Django) rely on unicode and for a good reason. I believe it would be a much better practice if the API would work with unicode objects without a problem. 

Please let us know If you think this error is on our part, if not, can you please add coverage for unicode objects. This problem keeps us stuck. 

Looking forward to your guidance. 
Thank you. 

---

To reproduce the problem
- Use Python 2.7
- Convert one of the address lines in your tests to unicode (as they contain none ascii Turkish chars) like so:

> `address = dict([('address', u'Nidakule Göztepe, Merdivenköy Mah. Bora Sok. No:1')])`
- Additionally you can convert other strings to unicode as well, to see that it works if the string doesn't contains none-ascii char.

issue_title: data_structures.py : DidlResource - add to_dict method
issue_body:
When using get_queue.to_dict, it returns a dict that has a 'resources' element that is a DidlResource object -- which is not serializable in json.  Have a to_dict method on the DidlResource would make life easier, and then get_queue's to_dict could call to_dict on the DidlResource.

I'll submit a patch but I have a question first.  When a DidlResource property is None, should the to_dict method return {'property_foo': None, ...}  or just not include the key in the returned dict?  I would think that the first option is preferable as it requires simpler logic to deal with key=>None than deal with a missing key.    Let me know and I'll start on the patch.   (I'll open a second issue on the get_queue.to_dict calling to_dict on the DidlResource, if/when this patch is accepted, because that might have other ramifications.)

issue_title: Add option to specify a on output file for cdxj
issue_body:
The current approach to generate the index file is to use it something like this:

```
$ ipwb index sample.warc > sample.cdxj
```

This works well, but there are situations where access to the `stdout` or redirecting it to a file is not easy. Also, when the indexer is being run along with some other things, the combined output on the `stdout` may generate an invalid index. This has been the case when I tried using it with Docker for example. Here is what I am looking for now, while keeping the default output on `stdout`, if `-o` or `--output` argument is passed with a filename (with any path), that file should be used for writing the index instead. Something like this:

```
$ ipwb index sample.warc -o sample.cdxj
```

issue_title: Improve banner design and usefulness
issue_body: The current replay banner (pictured) has a few issue that should be improved: - [x] Datetime is an awkward, long, numerical string - [x] URI-R and datetime are text _editable_ text fields - [x] Alignment of "Archived at:" and datetime has a lot of awkwardly unused space I know https://github.com/oduwsdl/reconstructive has much more capability than this. Can we look into taking advantage of this capability, @ibnesayeed?

issue_title: Provide further instructions for installing and usage as a binary from source

The instructions on accomplishing this are not yet complete in the README and cater more to the scenario of installing via via from pypi.

issue_title: Missing or misaligned table data
issue_body:
Hi,

I am doing a query on a sas table:

```
tbl = 'SCRAP'
libref = 'engnr_cl'
where = 'scrap_time >= '"01may2017 00:00"dt'

tempDf = s.sd2df(table=tbl, libref=libref, dsopts={'where': where})
```


and every row of the _SCRAP_TIME_ column should contain a datetime, however for some reason saspy or pandas is not treating this column as a datetime column - it is treating it as an "object". It's doing the same thing for some other datetime columns also.

When I look at the same time period in Enterprise Guide I can see datetimes in every row.

Here's an excerpt from the log (I cut out some columns as there are a lot of them):

```
data _null_; 
set engnr_cl.scrap(where=(scrap_time >= "01may2017 00:00"dt) );
file _tomods1; 
put 'AREA'n '09'x 
'LOTID'n '09'x 
'STEP_START_TIME'n E8601DT26.6 '09'x 
'SCRAP_TIME'n E8601DT26.6 '09'x
'STEP_END_TIME'n E8601DT26.6 '09'x 
'Comments'n '09'x
'LOT_TYPE'n '09'x 
'WEEK_NO'n '09'x 
'root_cause_location'n '09'x 
'Elec_Cause'n ;
run;
```

The log says 59 records were read.

I have tried filling the _NaN_ values in the _SCRAP_TIME_ column with _NaT_, but it still thinks there are blank strings in some of the cells.

The _Comments_ column contains text and each cell can contain multiple lines of text, probably separated by _newline_ characters (but I'm not sure about that). 

I tried the query below with and without the Comments column and it seemed to be okay without it, but the rows looked misaligned with the Comments column included.

`s.sd2df('SCRAP', 'ENGNR_CL', {'keep':'LOTID AREA SCRAP_TIME Comments', 'where':'SCRAP_TIME >= "01may2017 00:00"dt'})`

![image](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/8472772/26065208/1e51faec-398b-11e7-94c6-2e817a174c8a.png)

In the screenshot below, the _AREA_ column should only contain a single word per cell but instead seems to contain text that should have been in the _Comments_ column.

Any idea how I can get around this?

Thanks.

issue_title: Invalid JSON messages
Process cannot update as-path with "announce route" command.
issue_body:
Updating the as-path in an `announce route` command from a process does not update the as-path in the BGP announced route. I got into this state by having the process send an incorrect as-path first [65000 65000], and then fixing it to subsequently send [65101 65101] without reloading exabgp. Here are log messages after fixing the `announce route`:
```
Sep 21 10:54:46 servicevm exabgp[22664]: Wed, 21 Sep 2016 10:54:46 | INFO     | 22664  | processes     | Command from process radsecproxy-services : announce route 192.0.2.3/32 next-hop self as-path [ 65101 65101 ]
Sep 21 10:54:46 servicevm exabgp[22664]: Wed, 21 Sep 2016 10:54:46 | INFO     | 22664  | reactor       | Route added to neighbor 192.0.2.1 local-ip 192.0.2.2 local-as 65101 peer-as 65100 router-id 192.0.2.2 family-allowed in-open : 192.0.2.3/32 next-hop 192.0.2.2 as-path [ 65101 65101 ]
Sep 21 10:54:47 servicevm exabgp[22664]: Wed, 21 Sep 2016 10:54:47 | INFO     | 22664  | reactor       | Performing dynamic route update
Sep 21 10:54:47 servicevm exabgp[22664]: Wed, 21 Sep 2016 10:54:47 | INFO     | 22664  | reactor       | Updated peers dynamic routes successfully
```
Here is exabgp re-establishing the BGP session, and sending out an update with the incorrect as-path:
```
Sep 21 10:55:20 servicevm exabgp[22664]: Wed, 21 Sep 2016 10:55:20 | INFO     | 22664  | network       | Connected to peer neighbor 192.0.2.1 local-ip 192.0.2.2 local-as 65101 peer-as 65100 router-id 192.0.2.2 family-allowed in-open (out)
Sep 21 10:55:20 servicevm exabgp[22664]: Wed, 21 Sep 2016 10:55:20 | INFO     | 22664  | network       | Peer   192.0.2.1 ASN 65100    out loop, peer reset, message [notification received (3,11)] error[UPDATE message error / Malformed AS_PATH / 0x40020A02020000FDE80000FDE8]
```

Note that in the error message coming back from the peer, 0x0000FDE80000FDE8 is [65000 6500]. Restarting exabgp fixed this. Is the as-path cached and not updated?

I'm running version 3.4.13 from the Ubuntu Xenial package.

issue_title: undefined symbol: PyFPE_jbuf on stock Ubuntu 17.10
issue_body:
Trying to import after a successful install gives:

```sh
    from cytoolz import (
  File "~/venv/lib/python3.6/site-packages/cytoolz/__init__.py", line 3, in <module>
    from .functoolz import *
ImportError:~/venv/lib/python3.6/site-packages/cytoolz/functoolz.cpython-36m-x86_64-linux-gnu.so: undefined symbol: PyFPE_jbuf
```

It appears that cytoolz was built against a cpython with `--with-fpectl` on, which causes the above error when run on a cpython that is built without the flag. Ubuntu had the flag in the stock python in 17.04, then removed the flag in 17.10

Here is the unresolved issue with Python devs discussing how to handle it: https://bugs.python.org/issue29137

Is it possible to release a cytoolz that is built against a cpython without the flag? I don't see the fpectl module imported in pytoolz. (I'm pretty clueless in the area, so pardon my ignorance).

Some other relevant discussions, which mostly suggest building your own cpython (or cytoolz) locally as the only known workaround:
- https://stackoverflow.com/a/47703373/8412986
- https://github.com/numpy/numpy/issues/8415
- https://github.com/ethereum/web3.py/issues/554

issue_title: Reading a XML BIF with isolated nodes
issue_body:
The XMLBIFReader class doesn't allow to return the model from a BIF XML file where there are isolated nodes. 

I can create the XMLBIFReader:
`bif = pgmpy.readwrite.XMLBIF.XMLBIFReader('diabetes.xml')`

but when trying to return the model:
`bif_model = bif.get_model()`

It returns an exception because is trying to load the CPD of the isolated node, but this node is not included in the BayesianModel:
 `ValueError: ('CPD defined on variable not in the model', <TabularCPD representing P(skin:1) at 0x7f669d905c10>)` arises.


Reviewing the  `XMLBIFReader.get_model()`, we can see that the Bayesian model is created from the edges: 

`model = BayesianModel(self.get_edges())`

As an isolated node has no edge by definition, I think that the BayesianModel can't know that an isolated node exists when adding the cpds to the BayesianModel.

I provide the following XML BIF as a MWE. It is a XML BIF learnt using WEKA BayesNet class.
[diabetes.zip](https://github.com/pgmpy/pgmpy/files/1449591/diabetes.zip)

I think that even when an isolated node is not the most useful thing to do, it is a nuisance that we can't load the rest of the network.

Execution environment.
pgmpy: 0.1.3
Python: 2.7
OS: Ubuntu 16.04

issue_title: mti sources are not handled correctly
issue_body:
I spent the afternoon debugging why I'm not getting any GSUB/GPOS/GDEF out of the pipeline, turned out to be because of https://github.com/googlei18n/fontmake/commit/9c2ae2d6ee984b82ab2db741fdccf800e20b4061

while fixing that, I also made a change to how mti sources are consumed: https://github.com/googlei18n/fontmake/commit/1e2a639788a8d06564d1fe46a43d2e1bed03bb14

that exposed another bug.  Copying from the commit message there:
```
Make mti-sources processing more strict
If --mti-sources is provided, then it MUST declare mti sources for
all masters, so don't silently fallback to automatic feature
generation.

In fact, this exposed a bug in fontmake.  When running against
NotoSansDevanagari sources, we now get:

>> Saving OTF for NotoSansDevanagari-Light
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/bin/fontmake", line 9, in <module>
    load_entry_point('fontmake==1.0', 'console_scripts', 'fontmake')()
  File "build/bdist.linux-x86_64/egg/fontmake/__main__.py", line 42, in main
  File "build/bdist.linux-x86_64/egg/fontmake/font_project.py", line 221, in run_from_glyphs
  File "build/bdist.linux-x86_64/egg/fontmake/font_project.py", line 258, in run_from_ufos
  File "build/bdist.linux-x86_64/egg/fontmake/font_project.py", line 113, in build_otfs
  File "build/bdist.linux-x86_64/egg/fontmake/font_project.py", line 157, in save_otfs
KeyError: 'NotoSansDevanagari-Light'

That is because the sources call this font Thin, and the plist has that
and the mti-sources are called that.  But fontmake ignored the name
of the master and replaced it with Light, and is looking for Light.

That should be fixed separately.
```

issue_title: Any user can run our three featured models in bulk via the CLI.
Documentation bug - instructions for running allennlp in docker wrong
issue_body:
In the top level README.md for this project (https://github.com/allenai/allennlp/blob/v0.5.0/README.md), it says, "Note that if you use the source installation, you won't be able to use the allennlp command but rather you'll need to run ./bin/allennlp."

However, there is no bin/allennlp anyhwere in the container. Instead, from /stage/allennlp, you run `python allennlpy run.py`.

issue_title: Add versioning to the running demo.
issue_body:
Presently, there is no way to tell what version of code a running copy of the demo is using.  Other software at AI2 has a `/info` route which gives an overview of the service.  For example, see https://www.semanticscholar.org/api/1/info (only available on our network).

Ideally our demo would have a `/info` route as well, with information such as the following:

```
{
  name: "AllenNLP Demo",
  startTime: "Fri, 10 Nov 2017 13:48:51 -0800",
  gitVersion: "a5350d6c1b6816ce62584e707e4bb933840db8e3",
  githubUrl: "http://github.com/allenai/scholar/commit/a5350d6c1b6816ce62584e707e4bb933840db8e3",
  gitCommitDate: "Fri, 10 Nov 2017 10:20:18 -0800",
}
```

We could store the `gitVersion` and `gitCommitDate` when building a Docker image.

issue_title: Implement batch prediction for SRL
issue_body:
I'm currently trying to compile a dataset of ~50M SRL-parsed sentences and was hoping to speed things up with batch processing. Unfortunately, I discovered that it's not yet implemented for SRL.

https://github.com/allenai/allennlp/blob/f700584ed1b72ab45215e405ed0d22564938d1cb/allennlp/service/predictors/semantic_role_labeler.py#L52

In the meantime, do you have any suggestions for speeding up SRL parsing ?

I've also tried running the `predict` command with `--cuda_device 0` on a Tesla K80, but checking `nvidia-smi ` it seems that there's some bottleneck preventing it from going above 25% GPU usage. Could the lack of batch processing create some overhead that's responsible for that, or am I missing something else?

Thanks!

issue_title: Sibelius Trills w/o wavy lines get corrupted on import / Trill w/o end is buggy
issue_body:
Take the following XML code, generated by Sibelius:

```
        <note>
            <pitch>
                <step>E</step>
                <octave>5</octave>
            </pitch>
            <duration>576</duration>
            <voice>1</voice>
            <type>eighth</type>
            <dot/>
            <staff>1</staff>
            <beam number='1'>begin</beam>
            <notations>
                <ornaments>
                    <trill-mark default-y='20'/>
                    <wavy-line type='start' number='1'/>
                    <wavy-line type='stop' number='1'/>
                </ornaments>
            </notations>
        </note>
```

This is supposed to indicate a note with a "tr" sign and NO wavy line.  Sibelius counterintuitively creates a start and immediate stop on wavy-line instead.

This gets imported into music21 as both the TrillMark expression and also a TrillExtension:

```
 <music21.expressions.TrillExtension <music21.note.Note E>>
```

But this TrillExtension only has one note in it, so it is never stopped, and runs the entire length of the piece.  If more than six of these appear in a file, then we'll have reused id-numbers for wavy-line marks which is invalid MusicXML.   So invalid that, as a "Somebody Else's Problem type of thing", it gives a segfault to MuseScore.

Two bugs to fix:

1) Parse the wavy-line "start" followed by "stop" properly (either discard, or put the same note as start and stop element.
2) Close all TrillExtensions in the form of <music21.expressions.TrillExtension <music21.note.Note E>> before the end of the piece (or before the ID is reused) OR alternatively, omit them.

issue_title: More modular feature finding
issue_body:
As discussed in #239 and recently in #244, I would like to open a discussion on making feature finding more modular and hopefully more flexible and readable. I had a look at the code and we could breakdown locate in the following steps:

```
<parameter checks>
invert(raw_image)  # I would prefer to put this in a process_func and remove it from locate
image = preprocess(raw_image, noise_size, smoothing_size, threshold)
coords = local_maxima(image, radius, percentile, margin)
filter(coords, minmass, maxsize)
refine(raw_image, coords, separation, max_iterations, engine)  #possibly with characterize
filter(coords, minmass, minsize, topn)
characterize(raw_image, image, coords, radius, threshold)
```

To make the API user-friendly, we can work with `function`, `_function` combinations respectively including and not including the parameter checks.

I wonder what the performance loss would be if we would remove characterization from `refine` and write a new (possible numba) `characterize`.

What is the best place to make the transition `ndarray` -> `DataFrame`?

issue_title: zero_terms_query appended to match_phrase query
issue_body:
There is a regression since 0.7.x where `zero_terms_query: none` is being appended to generated query for `match_phrase`

For query `some_id: hello-world`

`0.6.1` generated `{'match_phrase': {'some_id': {'query': hello-world'}}}`
`0.7.1` generated `{'match_phrase': {'participant_id': {'query': 'LB-S00133', 'zero_terms_query': 'none'}}}`

Note that the 0.7.1 query fails on elasticsearch `5.6.8` with: `TransportError(400, 'parsing_exception', '[match_phrase] query does not support [zero_terms_query]')`

issue_body:
```
$ python
Python 3.6.1 (default, Mar 27 2017, 00:25:54)
>>> import tableschema
>>> open("bla.csv", "w").write("""id1,id2,value
... a,a,1
... a,b,2
... b,a,3
... b,b,4
... """)
38
>>> t=tableschema.Table("bla.csv", schema={
... "fields":[{"name":"id1", "type":"string"}, {"name":"id2", "type":"string"}, {"name":"value","type":"integer"}], "primaryKey": ["id1", "id2"]})
<tableschema.table.Table object at 0x10ddfc898>
>>> it=t.iter()
>>> list(it)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/Users/adam/.pyenv/versions/3.6.1/lib/python3.6/site-packages/tableschema/table.py", line 102, in iter
    raise exceptions.CastError(message)
tableschema.exceptions.CastError: Field "id1" duplicates in row "3"
```

Since `id1` and `id2` are the primary key _together_, the uniqueness constraint should not be imposed on each one of these columns.

issue_title: Unpivot  tabular data
issue_body:
As a dpp user, I want to unpivot or transpose my tabular data so that there's only one record per row

As a dpp user, I have data that has dates as  column names and I want to "unpivot" and have nice time series data so that I can easily reuse it for plotting graphs

Example:

```
# Pivoted data
country  	2005	2006	2007	
Afghanistan	46.6	46.9	47.2	
Albania	        76.1	76.3	76.5	
...

# Unpivoted data
country      year  average_age
Afghanistan  2005  46.6
Afghanistan  2006  46.9
Afghanistan  2007  47.2
Albania      2005  76.1
Albania      2006  76.3
Albania      2007  76.5
...
```

## Acceptance criteria

* [ ] Able to normalize my pivoted data
* [ ] `dpp run un-pivot-processor` provides valid datapackage 
* [ ] Able to pre define the name of columns to insert values

## Tasks

* [x] Do analysis
* [x] Prepare Spec
* [x] Failing Tests for processor
* [x] Create unpivot processor
* [ ] Passing test
* [ ] update REAMDE

## Analysis 

Example Spec for given data:
```
# data
country,2015 income,2015 expenses,2016 income,2016 expenses
xxx,20000,15000,30000,30000
...

# spec
- run: unpivot
  parameters:
    resources: (same as always)
    extraKeyFields:
      -
        name: year
        type: integer
      -
        name: direction
        name: string
    extraValueField:
        name: amount
        type: number
    unpivot:
      - 
        name: 2015 incomes
        keys:
          year: 2015
          direction: In
      - 
        name: 2015 expenses
        keys:
          year: 2015
          direction: Out
      - 
        name: 2016 incomes
        keys:
          year: 2016
          direction: In
      - 
        name: 2016 expenses
        keys:
          year: 2015
          direction: Out
      # or regex 
      -
        name: ([0-9]{4}) (\w+)
        keys:
          year: \1
          direction: \2
```

issue_title: Python 2.6 is being abandoned
issue_body:
Python 2.6 support is being dropped from openstack. As of Juno, openstack requires python 2.7. We're still trying to support Icehouse, which could optionally run with 2.6.

The first problem we've encountered is a dictionary comprehension is oslo messaging, which causes our `py26` tests to fail. Oslo messaging is no longer tested against python 2.6. https://github.com/openstack/oslo.messaging/commit/2734fc8f505ca07ff1a4f4a420807e5c1b4ed812

We need to set upper bounds on the `requirements.txt` and `testrequirements.txt` for python 2.6. We can change the requirements for `py26` tests in `tox.ini`. I don't know how we can change the requirements for the library for python 2.6,
